apis_used: External: Revver API POST /Token (OAuth ROPC grant_type=password)
External: Revver API POST /api/OneTimeToken (short-lived download token)
External: Revver API GET /api/FileDownload?id={fileId}&accessToken={oneTimeToken} (query-style fallback on 404)

root_causes_identified.description: download_file issues the final GET /api/FileDownload via a raw httpx.AsyncClient instead of _make_request_with_retry, so a 401 on the download URL bypasses the re-auth-and-retry branch entirely
root_causes_identified.affects: All Revver file downloads -- any 401 on FileDownload is terminal for that attempt

root_causes_identified.description: The only status-specific recovery inside download_file is a 404 path-to-query URL fallback; 401/403/429/5xx all fall through directly to raise_for_status()
root_causes_identified.affects: No server-driven recovery on the FileDownload GET except for 404

root_causes_identified.description: The pre-download token guard checks only the local _token_expiration clock; it does not validate the access token with Revver before reusing a cached fetcher
root_causes_identified.affects: A server-side revoked / invalidated access token continues to be sent as Bearer until its local clock expires

root_causes_identified.description: download_file still sends Authorization: Bearer <access_token> even though the OneTimeToken is embedded in the URL, so a bad access token causes 401 on the download regardless of OneTimeToken validity
root_causes_identified.affects: Couples FileDownload success to access-token validity instead of OneTimeToken validity alone

root_causes_identified.description: process_batch clears _fetcher_cache at the start of every batch and file_worker_job instantiates a brand-new FileProcessingWorkerService on every APScheduler firing, forcing a fresh OAuth handshake per Revver site per batch per worker
root_causes_identified.affects: Token-state lifecycle is tied to batch duration; multiplied by N parallel workers (PDMI prod=4, SKLSI prod=10)

root_causes_identified.description: Parallel worker instances each own an independent _fetcher_cache and issue independent OAuth handshakes; _auth_lock is a ClassVar asyncio.Lock that only serialises within a single process/event loop
root_causes_identified.affects: Multiple concurrent OAuth handshakes against the same Revver account from parallel workers are not serialised across processes or event loops

description: Traces the per-file Revver download flow from the APScheduler worker job through OAuth, OneTimeToken acquisition, and the final /api/FileDownload GET -- identifying that the final GET bypasses the _make_request_with_retry re-auth branch and that cached fetchers cannot detect a server-side token revocation, which together leave 401 responses on the FileDownload URL structurally unrecoverable within a single download attempt.
